violations.details: Parent receives command: str and order_data: dict. ParseCommand returns a string (validated command), but there is no child that takes this string and routes to the appropriate handler. The parent must use the output of ParseCommand to decide which child (PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, TrackOrder) to call, but the children PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, TrackOrder all expect order_data: dict, not the command string. The parent cannot pass the command string to these children, and there is no child that maps the command to the correct handler.

composition_feedback.suggested_fix: Add a routing child that takes the validated command string and returns the name of the handler to call, or modify the parent to directly use the command string to call the appropriate child. Alternatively, merge ParseCommand's logic into the parent.
